description: Platform identity + trust root. Singleton per deployment. Carries the platform issuer, per-service trust anchors (inline JWKS for origin/mantle/chorus), and the single-use bootstrap token hash. Created by Mantle on first boot from the init-container manifest at `${KEYS_DIR}/authority.manifest.json`. Mutable only by holders of the platform-admin grant on this artifact.

title.description: Display title for the authority artifact.

authority.description: Platform identity envelope. Public; readable by all authenticated users.

issuer.description: Platform issuer URL (the `iss` claim in all Origin-issued JWTs).

trust_anchors.description: Per-service trust anchor: URI + inline JWKS. Each entry corresponds to a service identity (origin, mantle, chorus). Inline JWKS is the canonical source — services do not need to fetch each other's /.well-known/jwks.json. Public; readable by all authenticated users (JWKS is public-key material).

jwks.description: RFC 7517 JSON Web Key Set: { keys: [ {kty, kid, n, e, alg, use} ] }

bootstrap_token_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the single-use bootstrap token. Cleared (set to null) after the first operator claims it via POST /auth/bootstrap/claim. Token has 256 bits of entropy and lives only in operator memory until claim, so a fast hash is sufficient — no bcrypt cost factor needed.

create.comment: The authority artifact is not creatable via the API. It is seeded by Mantle on first boot from the init-container manifest.

update.comment: Mutating the authority artifact (rotating JWKS, changing service URIs, clearing bootstrap_token_hash) requires the platform-admin grant (can_admin on the authority artifact itself). Services watch this event and refresh their cached trust map.

delete.comment: The authority artifact is the singleton trust root. It cannot be deleted.
